title: Rate limiter key is attacker-controlled without reverse proxy
description: server.rs:677-688: the inbox rate limiter uses X-Real-IP or X-Forwarded-For as the per-IP key. These headers are set by the client if there is no reverse proxy stripping them. With no proxy: (a) attacker sets X-Real-IP to a victim server's IP to exhaust that IP's rate limit (availability against legitimate federation), or (b) attacker sends a unique spoofed IP with every request to completely bypass rate limiting. There is no config option to either require a trusted proxy or fall back to the TCP peer address. The comment acknowledges the issue but there is no mitigation when the server is directly internet-exposed.
close_reason: Duplicate of broadside-zzj3.26 (same rate limiter X-Forwarded-For spoofing finding).
